strengths.label: Ambiguity to structured direction
strengths.description: Takes diffuse inputs or unclear problem spaces and transforms them into coherent roadmaps, requirements, or decision-ready assessments.

strengths.label: Cross-functional leadership without authority
strengths.description: Aligns business, technology, operations, and control functions through influence, coordination, and diplomacy rather than hierarchy.

strengths.label: Governance, data integrity, and operational rigour
strengths.description: Strengthens first-line controls, improves reporting accuracy, remediates audit issues, and increases process robustness.

strengths.label: Designing clarity in complex systems
strengths.description: Creates single sources of truth, simplifies workflows, and consolidates platforms. Strong preference for order, structure, and systemic coherence.

strengths.label: Executive communication and analytical output
strengths.description: Produces dashboards, KPI reports, and strategy packs that support senior decision-making and organisational alignment.

strengths.label: Multi-region and cross-cultural navigation
strengths.description: Comfortable working across Asia, Europe, and global teams with diverse stakeholders and varying governance environments.

core: Translating business needs into delivery plans
supporting: Front-office workflow support (RM tooling, sales enablement)
Regional portfolio oversight and multi-jurisdiction coordination

roles_that_fit: Product Owner / Product Lead for internal platforms (CRM, Sales, Workflow, Data Governance)
Business Manager / COO Office roles with multi-team or multi-region scope
Governance, reporting transparency, and operational improvement roles
Structured alignment roles across business and technical stakeholders
Roles requiring structured decision support and roadmap clarity
roles_to_avoid: Highly technical engineering or architecture positions
Pure strategy or innovation roles detached from operational realities
Early-stage startup roles lacking governance structures
Creative product ideation roles with minimal process or complexity
what_is_negotiable: Industry domain (skills transferable across regulated environments)
Reporting line structure (business, COO, or product-led)
Organisation size and maturity (governance must exist)

role_requirements: Mid-Senior to Director level only — no entry-level roles
Structural clarity and meaningful scope — not narrow task execution
Cross-functional collaboration as a core part of the work
Limited or no demand for hands-on technical engineering
work_model: Direct stakeholder and leadership interaction required — remote-only is a poor fit
environment: Must have structured governance and cross-functional mandates
Domains with minimal structure or weak governance are a poor match

work_preferences: Aligning multiple teams, systems, or jurisdictions
Improving decision-making through data, reporting, and governance
Coordinating across business and technology rather than working solo
Delivering platforms or processes that support front-office or operational teams
Solving problems involving both workflow and data complexity
